tables.title: Synchronized Cardioversion Energy by Rhythm (2025 AHA)
rows: After failed vagal maneuvers and adenosine
May need higher energies on subsequent shocks
Assume VT for all wide-complex regular tachycardias

tables.title: Antiarrhythmic Drugs for Tachycardia With a Pulse
rows: 6 mg rapid IV, then 12 mg (repeat once)
Proximal port + rapid flush; avoid in AF/flutter for rate control
150 mg IV over 10 min (may repeat once)
First-line for regular wide-complex; also used for AF rate control
Watch for hypotension; avoid in structural heart disease
0.25 mg/kg IV over 2 min, then infusion
Calcium-channel blocker; avoid in pre-excited AF
Beta-blocker; avoid in decompensated heart failure
Specific for polymorphic VT with prolonged QT

tables.title: Narrow vs Wide vs Irregular Tachycardia — Comparison
rows: Defibrillate if poly VT; avoid AV-nodal blockers if pre-excited AF
Unsynchronized defibrillation (poly VT) or synchronized cardioversion
AV-nodal blockers in pre-excited AF → VF
